page: Vultr vx1-m-192c-1536g-11520s is a high-capacity instance within the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by AMD processors, delivering 192 physical cores and 192 vCPUs with a single thread per core configuration. The CPU allocation is shared. This server is equipped with 1430.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 7.45 GB of RAM per core.

For storage-intensive workloads, the instance includes 10720 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It does not feature any dedicated GPU hardware, making it unsuitable for hardware-accelerated graphics or GPU-based machine learning. The network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

The shared CPU allocation presents a potential tradeoff in environments requiring sustained, isolated CPU performance, though the sheer scale of 192 cores mitigates resource constraints for many parallel tasks. This hardware profile is highly suited for large-scale databases, memory-intensive data processing, big data analytics, and enterprise applications requiring substantial local NVMe storage and memory capacity.
description: Vultr vx1-m-192c-1536g-11520s is a large-scale instance in the VX1 Cloud Compute family, built on the x86_64 architecture with AMD processors. It provides 192 vCPUs and 192 physical cores under a shared CPU allocation model, with one thread per core. The system is configured with 1430.0 GB of RAM, yielding 7.45 GB of memory per core, and features 10720 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. No GPU resources are included. This resource density makes the instance highly cost-efficient for memory-heavy and storage-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores. It is optimized for large databases, big data analytics, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.

tagline: A high-capacity x86_64 instance featuring 192 AMD cores, 1430 GB RAM, and 10.7 TB of local NVMe SSD storage.
